bull.b: A service-first model (per-scan/subscription plus software/services) supports recurring revenue, higher customer lifetime value, and predictable utilization-driven cash flow as deployments scale. This structural model aligns incentives for usage growth and cross-sell of AI and IT services over years.

Very low leverage gives the company financial flexibility to fund near-term deployment, R&D and restructuring without high interest burdens. This reduces bankruptcy pressure from fixed claims and provides room to raise capital or absorb temporary losses while commercial traction is pursued.

bull.t: Large Partner Pipeline And Operational Deployments
bull.b: A multi-hundred unit partner pipeline and an operational RadNet reference site indicate commercial validation and distribution reach. If converted, partner-led deployments enable scale for recurring service fees and software cross-sales, strengthening long-term revenue base and barrier to entry through installed ecosys

bear.t: Persistent Negative Gross Profit And Losses
bear.b: Ongoing negative gross margins mean core offerings don't yet cover direct costs, preventing operating leverage. Even with revenue growth, sustained gross losses require structural cost reductions, pricing changes, or product improvements before the business can generate durable operating profits.

bear.t: High Cash Burn And Weak Cash Generation
bear.b: Large and persistent negative OCF/FCF forces continual external financing, diluting shareholders and creating runway risk. Cash-intensive commercialization timelines and capex for deployments mean execution depends on capital markets access, making operational plans vulnerable if funding conditions tighten.

bear.t: Guidance Withdrawal And Delayed Revenue Recognition
bear.b: Removing annual guidance and citing variable install-to-revenue timelines signals execution risk converting agreements into recognized sales. This structural uncertainty complicates capacity planning, cash-flow forecasting and investor confidence until conversion timing is consistently demonstrated.
